content_markdown: A system consists of an ice cube placed in a cup of water. The system is thermally insulated from its surroundings. The water is originally at $20^{\circ}\text{C}$. Which graph best shows the variation of total internal energy $U$ of the system with time $t$?

diagram_metadata.description: Four graphs showing internal energy U versus time t. Graph A shows a horizontal line. Graph B shows an increasing curve with increasing gradient. Graph C shows a decreasing curve with decreasing gradient. Graph D shows an increasing straight line.

options.label: A
options.text: A horizontal line showing constant internal energy over time.

options.label: B
options.text: A curve showing internal energy increasing at an increasing rate over time.

options.label: C
options.text: A curve showing internal energy decreasing over time.

options.label: D
options.text: A straight line showing internal energy increasing at a constant rate over time.

vibe_explanation: The key phrase is 'thermally insulated from its surroundings'. According to the First Law of Thermodynamics, if no heat enters or leaves the system ($Q=0$) and no work is done on or by the system ($W=0$), the change in internal energy $\Delta U$ must be zero. Therefore, the total internal energy $U$ remains constant regardless of the phase changes or temperature changes occurring internally between the ice and the water.
